title: Ashtadhyayi
edition_note: Pilot passages only. Verify against the edition you choose before using this as a public benchmark release.

passages.translation_or_note: The vowels ā, ai, and au receive the technical designation vrddhi.
passages.grader_use: Use for the technical definition of vrddhi.

passages.translation_or_note: The vowels a, e, and o receive the technical designation guna in the relevant grammatical interpretation.
passages.grader_use: Use for the technical definition of guna.

passages.translation_or_note: A word denotes its own form, except where it functions as a non-word technical designation.
passages.grader_use: Use for questions about metalinguistic denotation and the samjna exception.

passages.translation_or_note: When there is conflict between applicable rules, the later rule is to be applied.
passages.grader_use: Use for grading explanations of rule conflict.

passages.translation_or_note: That which ends in a sup or tin ending is called a pada.
passages.grader_use: Use for grading pada definition questions.

passages.translation_or_note: After a or ā, a following simple vowel may produce the corresponding guna vowel in the relevant environment.
passages.grader_use: Use for simple guna sandhi derivations such as deva + indra -> devendra.
